gamma_events.title: Highest temperature in Munich on June 16?
gamma_events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 16 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ensemble forecasts from European models show Munich under a stable high-pressure regime with light westerly flow and variable cloud cover, limiting peak insolation and capping daytime maxima near 21–23 °C. This range aligns with climatological June averages of 21–22 °C and explains why traders assign the highest implied probabilities (37 %, 27.5 %, 25 %) to those exact thresholds. Minor differences hinge on afternoon convective timing and boundary-layer mixing depth, which can shift the daily high by 1–2 °C. Official DWD observations through midday will further refine the market as resolution nears.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model consensus from European forecasting centers points to a Munich maximum of 26–27°C on June 17, driven by moderate southwesterly flow, partial cloud cover limiting daytime heating, and surface dew points near 12°C that cap afternoon temperatures. Ensemble spreads remain narrow because the ridge axis is well-established, though a slight northward shift in the thermal low could allow brief clearing and push readings toward 28°C. Traders therefore assign the highest implied probabilities to 26°C and 27°C, reflecting the narrow range typical for mid-June under settled anticyclonic conditions with minimal advection of warmer air masses. Updated DWD and ECMWF runs tomorrow morning will refine the final maximum before resolution.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 18 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Munich Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models from sources including the Met Office, BBC Weather, and AccuWeather show Munich highs on June 18 likely reaching 29–32°C amid building high pressure and southerly flow, with ensemble guidance indicating modest warming potential but notable spread in peak values. Official guidance highlights mostly sunny conditions and light winds that favor maximum temperatures near the 30°C threshold, though afternoon convective activity or slight timing shifts in the ridge could trim or boost readings by 1–2°C. Trader consensus clusters on 30–31°C outcomes, reflecting the narrow scientific uncertainty window typical two days ahead, where updated runs from European and global models will refine land-surface heating and boundary-layer details before resolution. Historical June averages near 22°C underscore the anomalous warmth currently signaled.
